name: Effect v4 Type: DateTime.DateTime
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.DateTime
Category: type
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: type
Category Tag: models

Signature: DateTime = Utc | Zoned

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

// Create a UTC DateTime
const utc: DateTime.DateTime = DateTime.nowUnsafe()

// Create a zoned DateTime
const zoned: DateTime.DateTime = DateTime.makeZonedUnsafe(new Date(), {
  timeZone: "Europe/London"
})
```

Since: 3.6.0

name: Effect v4 Type: DateTime.Utc
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.Utc
Category: type
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: interface
Category Tag: models

Signature: Utc extends DateTime.Proto { readonly _tag: "Utc" readonly epochMillis: number partsUtc: DateTime.PartsWithWeekday | undefined }

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const utc = DateTime.nowUnsafe()

if (DateTime.isUtc(utc)) {
  console.log(utc._tag) // "Utc"
  console.log(utc.epochMillis) // timestamp in milliseconds
}
```

Since: 3.6.0

name: Effect v4 Type: DateTime.Zoned
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.Zoned
Category: type
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: interface
Category Tag: models

Signature: Zoned extends DateTime.Proto { readonly _tag: "Zoned" readonly epochMillis: number readonly zone: TimeZone adjustedEpochMillis: number | undefined

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const zoned = DateTime.makeZonedUnsafe(new Date(), {
  timeZone: "Europe/London"
})

if (DateTime.isZoned(zoned)) {
  console.log(zoned._tag) // "Zoned"
  console.log(zoned.epochMillis) // timestamp in milliseconds
  console.log(DateTime.zoneToString(zoned.zone)) // "Europe/London"
}
```

Since: 3.6.0

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.Equivalence
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.Equivalence
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: instances

Description: An `Equivalence` for comparing two `DateTime` values for equality. Two `DateTime` values are considered equivalent if they represent the same point in time, regardless of their time zone.

Signature: Equivalence: Equ.Equivalence<DateTime> = Internal.Equivalence

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const utc = DateTime.makeUnsafe("2024-01-01T12:00:00Z")
const zoned = DateTime.makeZonedUnsafe("2024-01-01T12:00:00Z", {
  timeZone: "Europe/London"
})

console.log(DateTime.Equivalence(utc, zoned)) // true
```

Since: 3.6.0

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.Order
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.Order
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: instances

Description: An `Order` for comparing and sorting `DateTime` values. `DateTime` values are ordered by their epoch milliseconds, so earlier times come before later times regardless of time zone.

Signature: Order: order.Order<DateTime> = Internal.Order

Example:
```ts
import { Array, DateTime } from "effect"

const dates = [
  DateTime.makeUnsafe("2024-03-01"),
  DateTime.makeUnsafe("2024-01-01"),
  DateTime.makeUnsafe("2024-02-01")
]

const sorted = Array.sort(dates, DateTime.Order)
// Results in chronological order: 2024-01-01, 2024-02-01, 2024-03-01
```

Since: 3.6.0

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.clamp
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.clamp
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: instances

Description: Clamp a `DateTime` between a minimum and maximum value. If the `DateTime` is before the minimum, the minimum is returned. If the `DateTime` is after the maximum, the maximum is returned. Otherwise, the original `DateTime` is returned.

Signature: clamp: { <Min extends DateTime, Max extends DateTime>( options: { readonly minimum: Min; readonly maximum: Max } ): <A extends DateTime>(self: A) => A | Min | Max <A extends DateTime, Min extends DateTime, Max extends DateTime>(

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const min = DateTime.makeUnsafe("2024-01-01")
const max = DateTime.makeUnsafe("2024-12-31")
const date = DateTime.makeUnsafe("2025-06-15")

const clamped = DateTime.clamp(date, { minimum: min, maximum: max })
// clamped equals max (2024-12-31)
```

Since: 3.6.0

```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const date = new Date("2024-01-01T12:00:00Z")
const dateTime = DateTime.fromDateUnsafe(date)

console.log(DateTime.formatIso(dateTime)) // "2024-01-01T12:00:00.000Z"
```

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.makeUnsafe
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.makeUnsafe
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: constructors

Description: Create a `DateTime` from one of the following: - A `DateTime` - A `Date` instance (invalid dates will throw an `IllegalArgumentError`) - The `number` of milliseconds since the Unix epoch - An object with the parts of a date - A `string` that can be parsed by `Date.parse`

Signature: makeUnsafe: <A extends DateTime.Input>(input: A) => DateTime.PreserveZone<A> = Internal.makeUnsafe

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

// from Date
DateTime.makeUnsafe(new Date())

// from parts
DateTime.makeUnsafe({ year: 2024 })

// from string
DateTime.makeUnsafe("2024-01-01")
```

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.makeZonedUnsafe
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.makeZonedUnsafe
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: constructors

Description: Create a `DateTime.Zoned` using `DateTime.makeUnsafe` and a time zone. The input is treated as UTC and then the time zone is attached, unless `adjustForTimeZone` is set to `true`. In that case, the input is treated as already in the time zone. When `adjustForTimeZone` is true and ambiguous times occur during DST transitions, the `disambiguation` option controls how to resolve the ambiguity: - `compatible` (default): Choose earlier time for repeated times, later for gaps - `earlier`: Always choose

Signature: makeZonedUnsafe: (input: DateTime.Input, options?: { readonly timeZone?: number | string | TimeZone | undefined readonly adjustForTimeZone?: boolean | undefined readonly disambiguation?: Disambiguation | undefined }) => Zoned = Internal.makeZonedUnsafe

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

DateTime.makeZonedUnsafe(new Date(), { timeZone: "Europe/London" })
```

Since: 3.6.0

In that case, the input is treated as already in the time zone. When `adjustForTimeZone` is true and ambiguous times occur during DST transitions, the `disambiguation` option controls how to resolve the ambiguity: - `compatible` (default): Choose earlier time for repeated times, later for gaps - `earlier`: Always choose the

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.make
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.make
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: constructors

Description: Create a `DateTime` from one of the following: - A `DateTime` - A `Date` instance (invalid dates will throw an `IllegalArgumentError`) - The `number` of milliseconds since the Unix epoch - An object with the parts of a date - A `string` that can be parsed by `Date.parse` If the input is invalid, `None` will be returned.

Signature: make: <A extends DateTime.Input>(input: A) => DateTime.PreserveZone<A> | undefined = Internal.make

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

// from Date
DateTime.make(new Date())

// from parts
DateTime.make({ year: 2024 })

// from string
DateTime.make("2024-01-01")
```

Since: 3.6.0

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.makeZonedFromString
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.makeZonedFromString
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: constructors

Description: Create a `DateTime.Zoned` from a string. It uses the format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sss+HH:MM[Time/Zone]`.

Signature: makeZonedFromString: (input: string) => Zoned | undefined = Internal.makeZonedFromString

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const result1 = DateTime.makeZonedFromString(
  "2024-01-01T12:00:00+02:00[Europe/Berlin]"
)
console.log(result1 !== undefined) // true

const result2 = DateTime.makeZonedFromString("2024-01-01T12:00:00Z")
console.log(result2 !== undefined) // true

const invalid = DateTime.makeZonedFromString("invalid")
console.log(invalid === undefined) // true
```

Since: 3.6.0

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.setZone
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.setZone
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: time zones

Description: Set the time zone of a `DateTime`, returning a new `DateTime.Zoned`.

Signature: setZone: { (zone: TimeZone, options?: { readonly adjustForTimeZone?: boolean | undefined readonly disambiguation?: Disambiguation | undefined }): (self: DateTime) => Zoned

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime, Effect } from "effect"

Effect.gen(function*() {
  const now = yield* DateTime.now
  const zone = DateTime.zoneMakeNamedUnsafe("Europe/London")

  // set the time zone
  const zoned: DateTime.Zoned = DateTime.setZone(now, zone)
})
```

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.setZoneOffset
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.setZoneOffset
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: time zones

Description: Add a fixed offset time zone to a `DateTime`. The offset is in milliseconds.

Signature: setZoneOffset: { (offset: number, options?: { readonly adjustForTimeZone?: boolean | undefined readonly disambiguation?: Disambiguation | undefined }): (self: DateTime) => Zoned

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime, Effect } from "effect"

Effect.gen(function*() {
  const now = yield* DateTime.now

  // set the offset time zone in milliseconds
  const zoned: DateTime.Zoned = DateTime.setZoneOffset(now, 3 * 60 * 60 * 1000)
})
```

Since: 3.6.0

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.zoneMakeNamedUnsafe
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.zoneMakeNamedUnsafe
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: time zones

Description: Attempt to create a named time zone from a IANA time zone identifier. If the time zone is invalid, an `IllegalArgumentError` will be thrown.

Signature: zoneMakeNamedUnsafe: (zoneId: string) => TimeZone.Named = Internal.zoneMakeNamedUnsafe

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const londonZone = DateTime.zoneMakeNamedUnsafe("Europe/London")
console.log(DateTime.zoneToString(londonZone)) // "Europe/London"

const tokyoZone = DateTime.zoneMakeNamedUnsafe("Asia/Tokyo")
console.log(DateTime.zoneToString(tokyoZone)) // "Asia/Tokyo"

// This would throw an IllegalArgumentError:
// DateTime.zoneMakeNamedUnsafe("Invalid/Zone")
```

Since: 3.6.0

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.zoneMakeOffset
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.zoneMakeOffset
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: time zones

Description: Create a fixed offset time zone. The offset is specified in milliseconds from UTC. Positive values are ahead of UTC, negative values are behind UTC.

Signature: zoneMakeOffset: (offset: number) => TimeZone.Offset = Internal.zoneMakeOffset

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

// Create a time zone with +3 hours offset
const zone = DateTime.zoneMakeOffset(3 * 60 * 60 * 1000)

const dt = DateTime.makeZonedUnsafe("2024-01-01T12:00:00Z", {
  timeZone: zone
})
```

Since: 3.6.0

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.zoneMakeNamed
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.zoneMakeNamed
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: time zones

Description: Create a named time zone from a IANA time zone identifier. If the time zone is invalid, `None` will be returned.

Signature: zoneMakeNamed: (zoneId: string) => TimeZone.Named | undefined = Internal.zoneMakeNamed

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const validZone = DateTime.zoneMakeNamed("Europe/London")
console.log(validZone !== undefined) // true

const invalidZone = DateTime.zoneMakeNamed("Invalid/Zone")
console.log(invalidZone === undefined) // true
```

Since: 3.6.0

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.zoneMakeNamedEffect
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.zoneMakeNamedEffect
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: time zones

Description: Create a named time zone from a IANA time zone identifier. If the time zone is invalid, it will fail with an `IllegalArgumentError`.

Signature: zoneMakeNamedEffect: (zoneId: string) => Effect.Effect<TimeZone.Named, IllegalArgumentError> = Internal.zoneMakeNamedEffect

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime, Effect } from "effect"

const program = Effect.gen(function*() {
  const zone = yield* DateTime.zoneMakeNamedEffect("Europe/London")
  const now = yield* DateTime.now
  return DateTime.setZone(now, zone)
})
```

Since: 3.6.0

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.zoneMakeLocal
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.zoneMakeLocal
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: time zones

Description: Create a named time zone from the system's local time zone. This uses the system's configured time zone, which may vary depending on the runtime environment.

Signature: zoneMakeLocal: () => TimeZone.Named = Internal.zoneMakeLocal

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const localZone = DateTime.zoneMakeLocal()
const now = DateTime.nowUnsafe()
const localTime = DateTime.setZone(now, localZone)

console.log(DateTime.formatIsoZoned(localTime))
```

Since: 3.6.0

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.zoneFromString
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.zoneFromString
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: time zones

Description: Try to parse a `TimeZone` from a string. Supports both IANA time zone identifiers and offset formats like "+03:00".

Signature: zoneFromString: (zone: string) => TimeZone | undefined = Internal.zoneFromString

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const namedZone = DateTime.zoneFromString("Europe/London")
const offsetZone = DateTime.zoneFromString("+03:00")
const invalid = DateTime.zoneFromString("invalid")

console.log(namedZone !== undefined) // true
console.log(offsetZone !== undefined) // true
console.log(invalid === undefined) // true
```

Since: 3.6.0

```ts
// Outputs "Europe/London"
```

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.distance
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.distance
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: comparisons

Description: Calculate the difference between two `DateTime` values, returning the number of milliseconds the `other` DateTime is from `self`. If `other` is *after* `self`, the result will be a positive number.

Signature: distance: { (other: DateTime): (self: DateTime) => number (self: DateTime, other: DateTime): number } = Internal.distance

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime, Effect } from "effect"

Effect.gen(function*() {
  const now = yield* DateTime.now
  const other = DateTime.add(now, { minutes: 1 })

  // returns 60000
  DateTime.distance(now, other)
})
```

Since: 3.6.0

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.distanceDurationResult
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.distanceDurationResult
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: comparisons

Description: Calculate the difference between two `DateTime` values. If the `other` DateTime is before `self`, the result will be a negative `Duration`, returned as a `Failure`. If the `other` DateTime is after `self`, the result will be a positive `Duration`, returned as a `Success`.

Signature: distanceDurationResult: { (other: DateTime): (self: DateTime) => Result.Result<Duration.Duration, Duration.Duration> (self: DateTime, other: DateTime): Result.Result<Duration.Duration, Duration.Duration> } = Internal.distanceDurationResult

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime, Effect } from "effect"

Effect.gen(function*() {
  const now = yield* DateTime.now
  const other = DateTime.add(now, { minutes: 1 })

  // returns Result.succeed(Duration.minutes(1))
  DateTime.distanceDurationResult(now, other)

  // returns Result.fail(Duration.minutes(1))
  DateTime.distanceDurationResult(other, now)
})
```

```ts
import { DateTime, Effect } from "effect"

})
```

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.toDateUtc
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.toDateUtc
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const dt = DateTime.makeZonedUnsafe("2024-01-01T12:00:00Z", {
  timeZone: "Europe/London"
})

const utcDate = DateTime.toDateUtc(dt)
console.log(utcDate.toISOString()) // "2024-01-01T12:00:00.000Z"
```

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.toDate
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.toDate
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: conversions

Description: Convert a `DateTime` to a `Date`, applying the time zone first. For `DateTime.Zoned`, this adjusts for the time zone before converting. For `DateTime.Utc`, this is equivalent to `toDateUtc`.

Signature: toDate: (self: DateTime) => Date = Internal.toDate

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const utc = DateTime.makeUnsafe("2024-01-01T12:00:00Z")
const zoned = DateTime.makeZonedUnsafe("2024-01-01T12:00:00Z", {
  timeZone: "Europe/London"
})

console.log(DateTime.toDate(utc).toISOString())
console.log(DateTime.toDate(zoned).toISOString())
```

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.zonedOffset
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.zonedOffset
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: conversions

Description: Calculate the time zone offset of a `DateTime.Zoned` in milliseconds. Returns the offset from UTC in milliseconds. Positive values indicate time zones ahead of UTC, negative values indicate time zones behind UTC.

Signature: zonedOffset: (self: Zoned) => number = Internal.zonedOffset

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const zoned = DateTime.makeZonedUnsafe("2024-01-01T12:00:00Z", {
  timeZone: "Europe/London"
})

const offset = DateTime.zonedOffset(zoned)
console.log(offset) // 0 (London is UTC+0 in winter)
```

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.zonedOffsetIso
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.zonedOffsetIso
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: conversions

Description: Format the time zone offset of a `DateTime.Zoned` as an ISO string. The offset is formatted as "±HH:MM".

Signature: zonedOffsetIso: (self: Zoned) => string = Internal.zonedOffsetIso

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const zoned = DateTime.makeZonedUnsafe("2024-01-01T12:00:00Z", {
  timeZone: DateTime.zoneMakeOffset(3 * 60 * 60 * 1000) // +3 hours
})

const offsetString = DateTime.zonedOffsetIso(zoned)
console.log(offsetString) // "+03:00"
```

Description: Get the milliseconds since the Unix epoch of a `DateTime`. This returns the UTC timestamp regardless of any time zone information.

Signature: toEpochMillis: (self: DateTime) => number = Internal.toEpochMillis

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const dt = DateTime.makeUnsafe("2024-01-01T00:00:00Z")
const epochMillis = DateTime.toEpochMillis(dt)

console.log(epochMillis) // 1704067200000
console.log(new Date(epochMillis).toISOString()) // "2024-01-01T00:00:00.000Z"
```

```ts
}).pipe(
  DateTime.removeTime,
```

name: Effect v4 Type: DateTime.CurrentTimeZone

episode_body:
Title: DateTime.setZoneCurrent
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: current time zone

Description: Set the time zone of a `DateTime` to the current time zone, which is determined by the `CurrentTimeZone` service.

```ts
import { DateTime, Effect } from "effect"

Effect.gen(function*() {
  const now = yield* DateTime.now

  // set the time zone to "Europe/London"
  const zoned = yield* DateTime.setZoneCurrent(now)
}).pipe(DateTime.withCurrentZoneNamed("Europe/London"))
```

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.mutate
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.mutate
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: mapping

Description: Modify a `DateTime` by applying a function to a cloned `Date` instance. The `Date` will first have the time zone applied if possible, and then be converted back to a `DateTime` within the same time zone. Supports `disambiguation` when the new wall clock time is ambiguous.

Signature: mutate: { ( f: (date: Date) => void, options?: { readonly disambiguation?: Disambiguation | undefined

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const dt = DateTime.makeUnsafe("2024-01-01T12:00:00Z")

const modified = DateTime.mutate(dt, (date) => {
  date.setHours(15) // Set to 3 PM
  date.setMinutes(30) // Set to 30 minutes
})

console.log(DateTime.formatIso(modified)) // "2024-01-01T15:30:00.000Z"
```

```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const dt = DateTime.makeZonedUnsafe("2024-01-01T12:00:00Z", {
  timeZone: "Europe/London"
})

const modified = DateTime.mutateUtc(dt, (date) => {
  date.setUTCHours(18) // Set UTC time to 6 PM
})

console.log(DateTime.formatIso(modified)) // "2024-01-01T18:00:00.000Z"
```

Category Tag: mapping

Description: Using the time zone adjusted `Date`, apply a function to the `Date` and return the result.

Signature: withDateUtc: { <A>(f: (date: Date) => A): (self: DateTime) => A <A>(self: DateTime, f: (date: Date) => A): A } = Internal.withDateUtc

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

// get the date in milliseconds
DateTime.makeUnsafe(0).pipe(
  DateTime.withDateUtc((date) => date.getTime())
)
```

Since: 3.6.0

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.match
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.match
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: mapping

Description: Pattern match on a `DateTime` to handle `Utc` and `Zoned` cases differently.

Signature: match: { <A, B>(options: { readonly onUtc: (_: Utc) => A readonly onZoned: (_: Zoned) => B }): (self: DateTime) => A | B

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const dt1 = DateTime.nowUnsafe() // Utc
const dt2 = DateTime.makeZonedUnsafe(new Date(), { timeZone: "Europe/London" }) // Zoned

const result1 = DateTime.match(dt1, {
  onUtc: (utc) => `UTC: ${DateTime.formatIso(utc)}`,
  onZoned: (zoned) => `Zoned: ${DateTime.formatIsoZoned(zoned)}`
})

const result2 = DateTime.match(dt2, {
  onUtc: (utc) => `UTC: ${DateTime.formatIso(utc)}`,
  onZoned: (zoned) => `Zoned: ${DateTime.formatIsoZoned(zoned)}`
})
```

Title: DateTime.startOf
Category: function

Description: Converts a `DateTime` to the start of the given `part`. If the part is `week`, the `weekStartsOn` option can be used to specify the day of the week that the week starts on. The default is 0 (Sunday).

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.format
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.format
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: formatting

Description: Format a `DateTime` as a string using the `DateTimeFormat` API. The `timeZone` option is set to the offset of the time zone. Note: On Node versions < 22, fixed "Offset" zones will set the time zone to "UTC" and use the adjusted `Date`.

Signature: format: { ( options?: | Intl.DateTimeFormatOptions & { readonly locale?: string | undefined

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const dt = DateTime.makeZonedUnsafe("2024-06-15T14:30:00Z", {
  timeZone: "Europe/London"
})

const formatted = DateTime.format(dt, {
  dateStyle: "full",
  timeStyle: "short",
  locale: "en-US"
})

console.log(formatted) // "Saturday, June 15, 2024 at 3:30 PM"
```

Since: 3.6.0

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.formatLocal
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.formatLocal
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: formatting

Description: Format a `DateTime` as a string using the `DateTimeFormat` API. It will use the system's local time zone & locale.

Signature: formatLocal: { ( options?: | Intl.DateTimeFormatOptions & { readonly locale?: string | undefined

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const dt = DateTime.makeUnsafe("2024-06-15T14:30:00Z")

// Uses system local time zone and locale
const local = DateTime.formatLocal(dt, {
  year: "numeric",
  month: "long",
  day: "numeric",
  hour: "2-digit",
  minute: "2-digit"
})

console.log(local) // Output depends on system locale/timezone
```

Since: 3.6.0

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.formatUtc
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.formatUtc
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: formatting

Description: Format a `DateTime` as a string using the `DateTimeFormat` API. This forces the time zone to be UTC.

Signature: formatUtc: { ( options?: | Intl.DateTimeFormatOptions & { readonly locale?: string | undefined

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const dt = DateTime.makeZonedUnsafe("2024-06-15T14:30:00Z", {
  timeZone: "Europe/London"
})

// Force UTC formatting regardless of time zone
const utcFormatted = DateTime.formatUtc(dt, {
  year: "numeric",
  month: "2-digit",
  day: "2-digit",
  hour: "2-digit",
  minute: "2-digit",
  timeZoneName: "short"
})

console.log(utcFormatted) // "06/15/2024, 02:30 PM UTC"
```

Since: 3.6.0

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.formatIntl
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.formatIntl
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: formatting

Description: Format a `DateTime` as a string using the `DateTimeFormat` API.

Signature: formatIntl: { (format: Intl.DateTimeFormat): (self: DateTime) => string (self: DateTime, format: Intl.DateTimeFormat): string } = Internal.formatIntl

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const dt = DateTime.makeUnsafe("2024-06-15T14:30:00Z")

// Create a custom formatter
const formatter = new Intl.DateTimeFormat("de-DE", {
  year: "numeric",
  month: "long",
  day: "numeric",
  hour: "2-digit",
  minute: "2-digit",
  timeZone: "Europe/Berlin"
})

const formatted = DateTime.formatIntl(dt, formatter)
console.log(formatted) // "15. June 2024, 16:30"
```

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.formatIso
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.formatIso
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: formatting

Description: Format a `DateTime` as a UTC ISO string. Always returns the UTC representation in ISO 8601 format, ignoring any time zone.

Signature: formatIso: (self: DateTime) => string = Internal.formatIso

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const dt = DateTime.makeUnsafe("2024-01-01T12:30:45.123Z")
console.log(DateTime.formatIso(dt)) // "2024-01-01T12:30:45.123Z"

const zoned = DateTime.makeZonedUnsafe("2024-01-01T12:30:45.123Z", {
  timeZone: "Europe/London"
})
console.log(DateTime.formatIso(zoned)) // "2024-01-01T12:30:45.123Z"
```

Since: 3.6.0

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.formatIsoDate
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.formatIsoDate
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: formatting

Description: Format a `DateTime` as a time zone adjusted ISO date string. Returns only the date part (YYYY-MM-DD) after applying time zone adjustments.

Signature: formatIsoDate: (self: DateTime) => string = Internal.formatIsoDate

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const dt = DateTime.makeUnsafe("2024-01-01T23:30:00Z")
console.log(DateTime.formatIsoDate(dt)) // "2024-01-01"

const zoned = DateTime.makeZonedUnsafe("2024-01-01T23:30:00Z", {
  timeZone: "Pacific/Auckland" // UTC+12/13
})
console.log(DateTime.formatIsoDate(zoned)) // "2024-01-02" (next day in Auckland)
```

```ts
console.log(DateTime.formatIsoDateUtc(zoned)) // "2024-01-01" (always UTC)
```

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.formatIsoOffset
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.formatIsoOffset
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: formatting

Description: Format a `DateTime.Zoned` as an ISO string with an offset. For `DateTime.Utc`, returns the same as `formatIso`. For `DateTime.Zoned`, includes the time zone offset in the format.

Signature: formatIsoOffset: (self: DateTime) => string = Internal.formatIsoOffset

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime } from "effect"

const utc = DateTime.makeUnsafe("2024-01-01T12:00:00Z")
console.log(DateTime.formatIsoOffset(utc)) // "2024-01-01T12:00:00.000Z"

const zoned = DateTime.makeZonedUnsafe("2024-01-01T12:00:00Z", {
  timeZone: DateTime.zoneMakeOffset(3 * 60 * 60 * 1000)
})
console.log(DateTime.formatIsoOffset(zoned)) // "2024-01-01T15:00:00.000+03:00"
```

  timeZone: DateTime.zoneMakeOffset(3 * 60 * 60 * 1000)
})

const offsetFormatted = DateTime.formatIsoZoned(offsetZone)
console.log(offsetFormatted) // "2024-06-

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.layerCurrentZone
Effect v4 Function: DateTime.layerCurrentZoneOffset
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.layerCurrentZoneOffset
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: current time zone

Description: Create a Layer from the given time zone offset. This layer provides the `CurrentTimeZone` service with a fixed offset time zone.

Signature: layerCurrentZoneOffset = (offset: number): Layer.Layer<CurrentTimeZone> => Layer.succeed(CurrentTimeZone)(Internal.zoneMakeOffset(offset))

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime, Effect } from "effect"

// Create a layer for UTC+3
const layer = DateTime.layerCurrentZoneOffset(3 * 60 * 60 * 1000)

const program = Effect.gen(function*() {
  const now = yield* DateTime.nowInCurrentZone
  return DateTime.formatIsoZoned(now)
})

Effect.provide(program, layer)
```

Since: 3.6.0

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.layerCurrentZoneNamed
episode_body:
Title: DateTime.layerCurrentZoneNamed
Category: function
Module Path: effect/DateTime
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: current time zone

Description: Create a Layer from the given IANA time zone identifier. This layer provides the `CurrentTimeZone` service with a named time zone. If the time zone identifier is invalid, the layer will fail.

Signature: layerCurrentZoneNamed: (zoneId: string) => Layer.Layer< CurrentTimeZone, IllegalArgumentError > = flow(Internal.zoneMakeNamedEffect, Layer.effect(CurrentTimeZone))

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime, Effect } from "effect"

const layer = DateTime.layerCurrentZoneNamed("Europe/London")

const program = Effect.gen(function*() {
  const now = yield* DateTime.nowInCurrentZone
  return DateTime.formatIsoZoned(now)
})

Effect.provide(program, layer)
```

name: Effect v4 Function: DateTime.layerCurrentZoneLocal

Title: DateTime.layerCurrentZoneLocal
Category: function

Export Kind: const

Description: Create a Layer from the system's local time zone. This layer provides the `CurrentTimeZone` service using the system's configured local time zone.

Example:
```ts
import { DateTime, Effect } from "effect"

const program = Effect.gen(function*() {
  const now = yield* DateTime.nowInCurrentZone
  return DateTime.formatIsoZoned(now)

// Use the system's local time zone
Effect.provide(program, DateTime.layerCurrentZoneLocal)
```

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.Array
episode_body:
Title: Array.Array
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: constructors

Description: Utilities for working with immutable arrays (and non-empty arrays) in a functional style. All functions treat arrays as immutable — they return new arrays rather than mutating the input. ## Mental model - **`Array<A>`** is a standard JS array. All functions in this module return new arrays; the input is never mutated. - **`NonEmptyReadonlyArray<A>`** (`readonly [A, ...Array<A>]`) is a readonly array guaranteed to have at least one element. Many functions preserve or require this guarantee at the

Signature: Array = globalThis.Array

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

const numbers = Array.make(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)

const doubled = Array.map(numbers, (n) => n * 2)
console.log(doubled) // [2, 4, 6, 8, 10]

const evens = Array.filter(numbers, (n) => n % 2 === 0)
console.log(evens) // [2, 4]

const sum = Array.reduce(numbers, 0, (acc, n) => acc + n)
console.log(sum) // 15
```

Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Type: Array.NonEmptyReadonlyArray
episode_body:
Title: Array.NonEmptyReadonlyArray
Category: type
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: type
Category Tag: models

Description: A readonly array guaranteed to have at least one element. Use this type when you need to ensure non-emptiness at the type level while preventing mutation. Many Array module functions accept or return this type.

Signature: NonEmptyReadonlyArray<A> = readonly [A, ...Array<A>]

Example:
```ts
import type { Array } from "effect"

const nonEmpty: Array.NonEmptyReadonlyArray<number> = [1, 2, 3]
const head: number = nonEmpty[0] // guaranteed to exist
```

Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Type: Array.NonEmptyArray
episode_body:
Title: Array.NonEmptyArray
Category: type
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: type
Category Tag: models

Description: A mutable array guaranteed to have at least one element. This is the mutable counterpart of NonEmptyReadonlyArray. Most Array module functions return `NonEmptyArray` when the result is guaranteed non-empty.

Signature: NonEmptyArray<A> = [A, ...Array<A>]

Example:
```ts
import type { Array } from "effect"

const nonEmpty: Array.NonEmptyArray<number> = [1, 2, 3]
nonEmpty.push(4)
```

Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.make
episode_body:
Title: Array.make
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: constructors

Description: Creates a `NonEmptyArray` from one or more elements. - Use when you have literal values and want a typed non-empty array. - The element type is inferred as the union of all arguments. - Always returns a `NonEmptyArray` since at least one argument is required.

Signature: make = <Elements extends NonEmptyArray<unknown>>( ...elements: Elements ): NonEmptyArray<Elements[number]> => elements

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

const result = Array.make(1, 2, 3)
console.log(result) // [1, 2, 3]
```

Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.allocate
episode_body:
Title: Array.allocate
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: constructors

Description: Creates a new `Array` of the specified length with all slots uninitialized. - Use when you need a pre-sized array and will fill it imperatively. - Elements are typed as `A | undefined` since slots are empty. - Prefer makeBy when you can compute each element from its index.

Signature: allocate = <A = never>(n: number): Array<A | undefined> => new Array(n)

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

const result = Array.allocate<number>(3)
console.log(result.length) // 3
```

Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.makeBy
episode_body:
Title: Array.makeBy
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: constructors

Description: Creates a `NonEmptyArray` of length `n` where element `i` is computed by `f(i)`. - Use when you need an array whose values depend on the index. - `n` is normalized to an integer >= 1 — always returns at least one element. - Dual: `Array.makeBy(5, f)` or `pipe(5, Array.makeBy(f))`.

Signature: makeBy: { <A>(f: (i: number) => A): (n: number) => NonEmptyArray<A> <A>(n: number, f: (i: number) => A): NonEmptyArray<A> } = dual(2, <A>(n: number, f: (i: number) => A) => { const max = Math.max(1, Math.floor(n))

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

const result = Array.makeBy(5, (n) => n * 2)
console.log(result) // [0, 2, 4, 6, 8]
```

Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.range
episode_body:
Title: Array.range
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: constructors

Description: Creates a `NonEmptyArray` containing a range of integers, inclusive on both ends. - Use when you need a sequence of consecutive integers. - If `start > end`, returns `[start]`. - Always returns a `NonEmptyArray`.

Signature: range = (start: number, end: number): NonEmptyArray<number> => start <= end ? makeBy(end - start + 1, (i) => start + i) : [start]

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

const result = Array.range(1, 3)
console.log(result) // [1, 2, 3]
```

Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.replicate
episode_body:
Title: Array.replicate
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: constructors

Description: Creates a `NonEmptyArray` containing a value repeated `n` times. - Use when you need multiple copies of the same value. - `n` is normalized to an integer >= 1 — always returns at least one element. - Dual: `Array.replicate("a", 3)` or `pipe("a", Array.replicate(3))`.

Signature: replicate: { (n: number): <A>(a: A) => NonEmptyArray<A> <A>(a: A, n: number): NonEmptyArray<A> } = dual(2, <A>(a: A, n: number): NonEmptyArray<A> => makeBy(n, () => a))

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

const result = Array.replicate("a", 3)
console.log(result) // ["a", "a", "a"]
```

Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.fromIterable
episode_body:
Title: Array.fromIterable
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: constructors

Description: Converts an `Iterable` to an `Array`. - If the input is already an array, returns it **by reference** (no copy). - Otherwise, creates a new array from the iterable. - Use copy if you need a fresh array even when the input is already an array.

Signature: fromIterable = <A>(collection: Iterable<A>): Array<A> => Array.isArray(collection) ? collection : Array.from(collection)

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

const result = Array.fromIterable(new Set([1, 2, 3]))
console.log(result) // [1, 2, 3]
```

Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.ensure
episode_body:
Title: Array.ensure
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: constructors

Description: Normalizes a value that is either a single element or an array into an array. - If the input is already an array, returns it by reference. - If the input is a single value, wraps it in a one-element array. - Useful for APIs that accept `A | Array<A>`.

Signature: ensure = <A>(self: ReadonlyArray<A> | A): Array<A> => Array.isArray(self) ? self : [self as A]

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

console.log(Array.ensure("a")) // ["a"]
console.log(Array.ensure(["a", "b", "c"])) // ["a", "b", "c"]
```

Since: 3.3.0

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.fromRecord
episode_body:
Title: Array.fromRecord
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: conversions

Description: Converts a record into an array of `[key, value]` tuples. - Key order follows `Object.entries` semantics. - Returns an empty array for an empty record.

Signature: fromRecord: <K extends string, A>(self: Readonly<Record<K, A>>) => Array<[K, A]> = Record.toEntries

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

const result = Array.fromRecord({ a: 1, b: 2, c: 3 })
console.log(result) // [["a", 1], ["b", 2], ["c", 3]]
```

Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.fromOption
episode_body:
Title: Array.fromOption
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: conversions

Description: Converts an `Option` to an array: `Some(a)` becomes `[a]`, `None` becomes `[]`.

Signature: fromOption: <A>(self: O.Option<A>) => Array<A> = O.toArray

Example:
```ts
import { Array, Option } from "effect"

console.log(Array.fromOption(O.some(1))) // [1]
console.log(Array.fromOption(O.none())) // []
```

Since: 2.0.0

Title: Array.match
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: pattern matching

Description: Pattern-matches on an array, handling empty and non-empty cases separately. - Use when you need to branch on whether an array has elements. - `onNonEmpty` receives a `NonEmptyReadonlyArray`. - Dual: data-first or data-last.

Signature: match: { <B, A, C = B>( options: { readonly onEmpty: LazyArg<B> readonly onNonEmpty: (self: NonEmptyReadonlyArray<A>) => C

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

const describe = Array.match({
  onEmpty: () => "empty",
  onNonEmpty: ([head, ...tail]) => `head: ${head}, tail: ${tail.length}`
})
console.log(describe([])) // "empty"
console.log(describe([1, 2, 3])) // "head: 1, tail: 2"
```

Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.matchLeft
episode_body:
Title: Array.matchLeft
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: pattern matching

Description: Pattern-matches on an array from the left, providing the first element and the remaining elements separately. - `onNonEmpty` receives `(head, tail)` where `tail` is the rest of the array. - Use when you want to process the first element differently from the rest.

Signature: matchLeft: { <B, A, C = B>( options: { readonly onEmpty: LazyArg<B> readonly onNonEmpty: (head: A, tail: Array<A>) => C

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

const matchLeft = Array.matchLeft({
  onEmpty: () => "empty",
  onNonEmpty: (head, tail) => `head: ${head}, tail: ${tail.length}`
})
console.log(matchLeft([])) // "empty"
console.log(matchLeft([1, 2, 3])) // "head: 1, tail: 2"
```

Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.matchRight
episode_body:
Title: Array.matchRight
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: pattern matching

Description: Pattern-matches on an array from the right, providing all elements except the last and the last element separately. - `onNonEmpty` receives `(init, last)` where `init` is everything but the last element. - Use when you want to process the last element differently from the rest.

Signature: matchRight: { <B, A, C = B>( options: { readonly onEmpty: LazyArg<B> readonly onNonEmpty: (init: Array<A>, last: A) => C

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

const matchRight = Array.matchRight({
  onEmpty: () => "empty",
  onNonEmpty: (init, last) => `init: ${init.length}, last: ${last}`
})
console.log(matchRight([])) // "empty"
console.log(matchRight([1, 2, 3])) // "init: 2, last: 3"
```

Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.prepend
episode_body:
Title: Array.prepend
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: concatenating

Description: Adds a single element to the front of an iterable, returning a `NonEmptyArray`. - Always returns a non-empty array. - Does not mutate the input.

Signature: prepend: { <B>(head: B): <A>(self: Iterable<A>) => NonEmptyArray<A | B> <A, B>(self: Iterable<A>, head: B): NonEmptyArray<A | B> } = dual(2, <A, B>(self: Iterable<A>, head: B): NonEmptyArray<A | B> => [head, ...self])

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

const result = Array.prepend([2, 3, 4], 1)
console.log(result) // [1, 2, 3, 4]
```

Module Path: effect/Array

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.scan
episode_body:
Title: Array.scan
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: folding

Description: Left-to-right fold that keeps every intermediate accumulator value. - The output length is `input.length + 1` (starts with the initial value). - Always returns a `NonEmptyArray` because the initial value is included. - Use reduce if you only need the final accumulated value.

Signature: scan: { <B, A>(b: B, f: (b: B, a: A) => B): (self: Iterable<A>) => NonEmptyArray<B> <A, B>(self: Iterable<A>, b: B, f: (b: B, a: A) => B): NonEmptyArray<B> } = dual(3, <A, B>(self: Iterable<A>, b: B, f: (b: B, a: A) => B): NonEmptyArray<B> => { const out: NonEmptyArray<B> = [b]

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

const result = Array.scan([1, 2, 3, 4], 0, (acc, value) => acc + value)
console.log(result) // [0, 1, 3, 6, 10]
```

Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.scanRight
episode_body:
Title: Array.scanRight
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: folding

Description: Right-to-left fold that keeps every intermediate accumulator value. - The output length is `input.length + 1` (ends with the initial value). - Always returns a `NonEmptyArray`.

Signature: scanRight: { <B, A>(b: B, f: (b: B, a: A) => B): (self: Iterable<A>) => NonEmptyArray<B> <A, B>(self: Iterable<A>, b: B, f: (b: B, a: A) => B): NonEmptyArray<B> } = dual(3, <A, B>(self: Iterable<A>, b: B, f: (b: B, a: A) => B): NonEmptyArray<B> => { const input = fromIterable(self)

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

const result = Array.scanRight([1, 2, 3, 4], 0, (acc, value) => acc + value)
console.log(result) // [10, 9, 7, 4, 0]
```

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.get
episode_body:
Title: Array.get
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: getters

Description: Safely reads an element at the given index, returning `O.some` or `O.none` if the index is out of bounds. - The index is floored to an integer. - Never throws.

Signature: get: { (index: number): <A>(self: ReadonlyArray<A>) => O.Option<A> <A>(self: ReadonlyArray<A>, index: number): O.Option<A> } = dual(2, <A>(self: ReadonlyArray<A>, index: number): O.Option<A> => { const i = Math.floor(index)

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

console.log(Array.get([1, 2, 3], 1)) // Some(2)
console.log(Array.get([1, 2, 3], 10)) // None
```

Title: Array.getUnsafe

Signature: getUnsafe: { (index: number): <A>(self: ReadonlyArray<A>) => A <A>(self: ReadonlyArray<A>, index: number): A } = dual(2, <A>(self: ReadonlyArray<A>, index: number): A => { const i = Math.floor(index)

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

// Array.getUnsafe([1, 2, 3], 10) // throws Error
```

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.span
episode_body:
Title: Array.span
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: splitting

Description: Splits an iterable into two arrays: the longest prefix where the predicate holds, and the remaining elements. - Equivalent to `[takeWhile(pred), dropWhile(pred)]` but more efficient (single pass). - Supports refinements for type narrowing of the prefix.

Signature: span: { <A, B extends A>( refinement: (a: NoInfer<A>, i: number) => a is B ): (self: Iterable<A>) => [init: Array<B>, rest: Array<Exclude<A, B>>] <A>(predicate: (a: NoInfer<A>, i: number) => boolean): (self: Iterable<A>) => [init: Array<A>, rest: Array<A>]

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

console.log(Array.span([1, 3, 2, 4, 5], (x) => x % 2 === 1)) // [[1, 3], [2, 4, 5]]
```

```ts
console.log(Array.insertAt(["a", "b", "c", "e"], 3, "d")) // ["a", "b", "c", "d", "e"]
```

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.sortWith
episode_body:
Title: Array.sortWith
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: elements

Description: Sorts an array by a derived key using a mapping function and an `Order` for that key. - Equivalent to `sort(Order.mapInput(order, f))` but more convenient. - Does not mutate the input.

Signature: sortWith: { <S extends Iterable<any>, B>( f: (a: ReadonlyArray.Infer<S>) => B, order: Order.Order<B> ): (self: S) => ReadonlyArray.With<S, ReadonlyArray.Infer<S>>

Example:
```ts
import { Array, Order } from "effect"

console.log(Array.sortWith(["aaa", "b", "cc"], (s) => s.length, Order.Number))
// ["b", "cc", "aaa"]
```

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.sortBy
episode_body:
Title: Array.sortBy
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: sorting

Description: Sorts an array by multiple `Order`s applied in sequence: the first order is used first; ties are broken by the second order, and so on. - Data-last only (returns a function). - Preserves `NonEmptyArray` in the return type.

Signature: sortBy = <S extends Iterable<any>>( ...orders: ReadonlyArray<Order.Order<ReadonlyArray.Infer<S>>> ) => { const sortByAll = sort(Order.combineAll(orders)) return (

Example:
```ts
import { Array, Order, pipe } from "effect"

const users = [
  { name: "Alice", age: 30 },
  { name: "Bob", age: 25 },
  { name: "Charlie", age: 30 }
]

const result = pipe(
  users,
  Array.sortBy(
    Order.mapInput(Order.Number, (user: (typeof users)[number]) => user.age),
    Order.mapInput(Order.String, (user: (typeof users)[number]) => user.name)
  )
)
console.log(result)
// [{ name: "Bob", age: 25 }, { name: "Alice", age: 30 }, { name: "Charlie", age: 30 }]
```

```ts
)

```

```ts
console.log(Array.window([1, 2, 3, 4, 5], 3)) // [[1, 2, 3], [2, 3, 4], [3, 4, 5]]
console.log(Array.window([1, 2, 3, 4, 5], 6)) // []
```

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.groupWith
episode_body:
Title: Array.groupWith
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: grouping

Description: Groups consecutive equal elements using a custom equivalence function. - Only groups **adjacent** elements — non-adjacent duplicates stay separate. - Requires a `NonEmptyReadonlyArray`.

Signature: groupWith: { <A>(isEquivalent: (self: A, that: A) => boolean): (self: NonEmptyReadonlyArray<A>) => NonEmptyArray<NonEmptyArray<A>> <A>(self: NonEmptyReadonlyArray<A>, isEquivalent: (self: A, that: A) => boolean): NonEmptyArray<NonEmptyArray<A>> } = dual( 2,

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

console.log(Array.groupWith(["a", "a", "b", "b", "b", "c", "a"], (x, y) => x === y))
// [["a", "a"], ["b", "b", "b"], ["c"], ["a"]]
```

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.group
episode_body:
Title: Array.group
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array

Category Tag: grouping

Description: Groups consecutive equal elements using `Equal.equivalence()`. - Only groups **adjacent** elements.

Signature: group: <A>(self: NonEmptyReadonlyArray<A>) => NonEmptyArray<NonEmptyArray<A>> = groupWith( Equal.asEquivalence() )

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

console.log(Array.group([1, 1, 2, 2, 2, 3, 1])) // [[1, 1], [2, 2, 2], [3], [1]]
```

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.groupBy
episode_body:
Title: Array.groupBy
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: grouping

Description: Groups elements into a record by a key-returning function. Each key maps to a `NonEmptyArray` of elements that produced that key. - Unlike group/groupWith, elements do not need to be adjacent to be grouped together. - Key function must return a `string` or `symbol`.

Signature: groupBy: { <A, K extends string | symbol>( f: (a: A) => K ): (self: Iterable<A>) => Record<Record.ReadonlyRecord.NonLiteralKey<K>, NonEmptyArray<A>> <A, K extends string | symbol>(

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

const people = [
  { name: "Alice", group: "A" },
  { name: "Bob", group: "B" },
  { name: "Charlie", group: "A" }
]

const result = Array.groupBy(people, (person) => person.group)
console.log(result)
// { A: [{ name: "Alice", group: "A" }, { name: "Charlie", group: "A" }], B: [{ name: "Bob", group: "B" }] }
```

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.intersectionWith
episode_body:
Title: Array.intersectionWith
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: elements

Description: Computes the intersection of two arrays using a custom equivalence. Order is determined by the first array.

Signature: intersectionWith = <A>(isEquivalent: (self: A, that: A) => boolean): { (that: Iterable<A>): (self: Iterable<A>) => Array<A> (self: Iterable<A>, that: Iterable<A>): Array<A> } => { const has = containsWith(isEquivalent)

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

const array1 = [{ id: 1 }, { id: 2 }, { id: 3 }]
const array2 = [{ id: 3 }, { id: 4 }, { id: 1 }]
const isEquivalent = (a: { id: number }, b: { id: number }) => a.id === b.id
console.log(Array.intersectionWith(isEquivalent)(array2)(array1)) // [{ id: 1 }, { id: 3 }]
```

Title: Array.partitionMap

Description: Maps each element to a `Result`, then separates failures and successes into two arrays. - Returns `[failures, successes]`.

Signature: partitionMap: { <A, B, C>(f: (a: A, i: number) => Result.Result<C, B>): (self: Iterable<A>) => [fails: Array<B>, successes: Array<C>] <A, B, C>(self: Iterable<A>, f: (a: A, i: number) => Result.Result<C, B>): [fails: Array<B>, successes: Array<C>] } = dual( 2,

Example:
```ts
import { Array, Result } from "effect"

const result = Array.partitionMap(
  [1, 2, 3, 4, 5],

)

```

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.liftOption
episode_body:
Title: Array.liftOption
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: lifting

Description: Lifts an `Option`-returning function into one that returns an array: `Some(a)` becomes `[a]`, `None` becomes `[]`.

Signature: liftOption = <A extends Array<unknown>, B>( f: (...a: A) => O.Option<B> ) => (...a: A): Array<B> => fromOption(f(...a))

Example:
```ts
import { Array, Option } from "effect"

const parseNumber = Array.liftOption((s: string) => {
  const n = Number(s)
  return isNaN(n) ? O.none() : O.some(n)
})
console.log(parseNumber("123")) // [123]
console.log(parseNumber("abc")) // []
```

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.liftResult
episode_body:
Title: Array.liftResult
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: lifting

Description: Lifts a `Result`-returning function into one that returns an array: failures produce `[]`, successes produce `[value]`.

Signature: liftResult = <A extends Array<unknown>, E, B>( f: (...a: A) => Result.Result<B, E> ) => (...a: A): Array<B> => { const e = f(...a)

Example:
```ts
import { Array, Result } from "effect"

const parseNumber = (s: string): Result.Result<number, Error> =>
  isNaN(Number(s))
    ? Result.fail(new Error("Not a number"))
    : Result.succeed(Number(s))

const liftedParseNumber = Array.liftResult(parseNumber)
console.log(liftedParseNumber("42")) // [42]
console.log(liftedParseNumber("not a number")) // []
```

episode_body:
Title: Array.mapAccum
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const

Description: Maps over an array while threading an accumulator through each step, returning both the final state and the mapped array. - Combines `map` and `reduce` in a single pass. - The callback receives the current state, element, and index, and returns `[nextState, mappedValue]`. - Returns `[finalState, mappedArray]`. - Dual: can be used in both data-first and data-last style.

Signature: mapAccum: { <S, A, B, I extends Iterable<A> = Iterable<A>>( s: S, f: (s: S, a: ReadonlyArray.Infer<I>, i: number) => readonly [S, B] ): (self: I) => [state: S, mappedArray: ReadonlyArray.With<I, B>]

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

const result = Array.mapAccum([1, 2, 3], 0, (acc, n) => [acc + n, acc + n])
console.log(result) // [6, [1, 3, 6]]
```

Title: Array.cartesianWith
Category: function

Description: Computes the cartesian product of two arrays, applying a combiner to each pair. - Produces every combination of an element from `self` with an element from `that`. - Result length is `self.length * that.length`. - Order: iterates `that` for each element of `self`.

Signature: cartesianWith: { <A, B, C>(that: ReadonlyArray<B>, f: (a: A, b: B) => C): (self: ReadonlyArray<A>) => Array<C> <A, B, C>(self: ReadonlyArray<A>, that: ReadonlyArray<B>, f: (a: A, b: B) => C): Array<C> } = dual( 3,

Example:
```ts
import { Array } from "effect"

const result = Array.cartesianWith([1, 2], ["a", "b"], (a, b) => `${a}-${b}`)
console.log(result) // ["1-a", "1-b", "2-a", "2-b"]
```

Signature: cartesian: { <B>(that: ReadonlyArray<B>): <A>(self: ReadonlyArray<A>) => Array<[A, B]> <A, B>(self: ReadonlyArray<A>, that: ReadonlyArray<B>): Array<[A, B]> } = dual( 2,

Example:

```ts
const result = Array.cartesian([1, 2], ["a", "b"])
console.log(result) // [[1, "a"], [1, "b"], [2, "a"], [2, "b"]]
```

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.Do
episode_body:
Title: Array.Do
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: do notation

Description: Starting point for the "do simulation" — an array comprehension pattern. - Begin a pipeline with `Do`, then use bind to introduce array variables and let for plain values. - Each `bind` produces the cartesian product of all bound variables (like nested loops). - Use `filter` and `map` in the pipeline to add conditions and transformations.

Signature: Do: ReadonlyArray<{}> = of({})

Example:
```ts
import { Array, pipe } from "effect"

const result = pipe(
  Array.Do,
  Array.bind("x", () => [1, 3, 5]),
  Array.bind("y", () => [2, 4, 6]),
  Array.filter(({ x, y }) => x < y),
  Array.map(({ x, y }) => [x, y] as const)
)
console.log(result) // [[1, 2], [1, 4], [1, 6], [3, 4], [3, 6], [5, 6]]
```

Since: 3.2.0

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.bind
episode_body:
Title: Array.bind
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: do notation

Description: Introduces a new array variable into a do-notation scope, producing the cartesian product with all previous bindings. - Each `bind` call adds a named property to the accumulated object. - The callback receives the current scope and must return an array. - Equivalent to `flatMap` + merging the new value into the scope object.

Signature: bind: { <A extends object, N extends string, B>( tag: Exclude<N, keyof A>, f: (a: NoInfer<A>) => ReadonlyArray<B> ): (

Example:
```ts
import { Array, pipe } from "effect"

const result = pipe(
  Array.Do,
  Array.bind("x", () => [1, 2]),
  Array.bind("y", () => ["a", "b"])
)
console.log(result)
// [{ x: 1, y: "a" }, { x: 1, y: "b" }, { x: 2, y: "a" }, { x: 2, y: "b" }]
```

Since: 3.2.0

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.bindTo
episode_body:
Title: Array.bindTo
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: do notation

Description: Names the elements of an array by wrapping each in an object with the given key, starting a do-notation scope. - Equivalent to `Array.map(self, (a) => ({ [tag]: a }))`. - Alternative to starting with `Do` + `bind`; useful when you already have an array.

Signature: bindTo: { <N extends string>(tag: N): <A>(self: ReadonlyArray<A>) => Array<{ [K in N]: A }> <A, N extends string>(self: ReadonlyArray<A>, tag: N): Array<{ [K in N]: A }> } = internalDoNotation.bindTo<ReadonlyArrayTypeLambda>(map) as any

Example:
```ts
import { Array, pipe } from "effect"

const result = pipe(
  [1, 2, 3],
  Array.bindTo("x")
)
console.log(result) // [{ x: 1 }, { x: 2 }, { x: 3 }]
```

Since: 3.2.0

name: Effect v4 Function: Array.getReadonlyReducerConcat
episode_body:
Title: Array.getReadonlyReducerConcat
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Array
Export Kind: function
Category Tag: do notation

Description: Adds a computed plain value to the do-notation scope without introducing a new array dimension. - Unlike bind, the callback returns a single value (not an array), so no cartesian product occurs. - Useful for derived or intermediate values that depend on previously bound variables.

Signature: getReadonlyReducerConcat<A>(): Reducer.Reducer<ReadonlyArray<A>> { return reducer }

Example:
```ts
import { Array, pipe } from "effect"

const result = pipe(
  Array.Do,
  Array.bind("x", () => [1, 2, 3]),
  Array.let("doubled", ({ x }) => x * 2)
)
console.log(result)
// [{ x: 1, doubled: 2 }, { x: 2, doubled: 4 }, { x: 3, doubled: 6 }]
```

Description: Atomically modifies the value of the Ref using the given partial function. The function receives the current value and returns an Option of [result, newValue]. If the function returns `O.some([result, newValue])`, the Ref is updated with newValue and result is returned. If it returns `O.none()`, the Ref is left unchanged and the fallback value is returned.

  )

```ts
import { Deferred, Effect, Exit } from "effect"

const program = Effect.gen(function*() {
  const deferred = yield* Deferred.make<number>()
  yield* Deferred.done(deferred, Exit.succeed(42))

  const value = yield* Deferred.await(deferred)
  console.log(value) // 42
})
```

name: Effect v4 Function: Deferred.isDone
episode_body:
Title: Deferred.isDone
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Deferred
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: getters

Description: Returns `true` if this `Deferred` has already been completed with a value or an error, `false` otherwise.

Signature: isDone = <A, E>(self: Deferred<A, E>): Effect<boolean> => internalEffect.sync(() => isDoneUnsafe(self))

Example:
```ts
import { Deferred, Effect } from "effect"

const program = Effect.gen(function*() {
  const deferred = yield* Deferred.make<number>()
  const beforeCompletion = yield* Deferred.isDone(deferred)
  console.log(beforeCompletion) // false

  yield* Deferred.succeed(deferred, 42)
  const afterCompletion = yield* Deferred.isDone(deferred)
  console.log(afterCompletion) // true
})
```

name: Effect v4 Function: O.none
episode_body:
Title: O.none
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Option
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: Constructors

Description: Creates an `Option` representing the absence of a value. **When to use** - Representing a missing or uninitialized value - Returning "no result" from a function **Behavior** - Returns `Option<never>`, which is a subtype of `Option<A>` for any `A` - Always returns the same singleton instance

Signature: none = <A = never>(): Option<A> => option.none

Example:
```ts
import { Option } from "effect"

//      ┌─── Option<never>
//      ▼
const noValue = O.none()

console.log(noValue)
// Output: { _id: 'Option', _tag: 'None' }
```

name: Effect v4 Function: O.some
episode_body:
Title: O.some
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Option
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: Constructors

Description: Wraps the given value into an `Option` to represent its presence. **When to use** - Wrapping a known-present value as `Option` - Returning a successful result from a partial function **Behavior** - Always returns `Some<A>` - Does not filter `null` or `undefined`; use fromNullishOr for that

Signature: some: <A>(value: A) => Option<A> = option.some

Example:
```ts
import { Option } from "effect"

//      ┌─── Option<number>
//      ▼
const value = O.some(1)

console.log(value)
// Output: { _id: 'Option', _tag: 'Some', value: 1 }
```

name: Effect v4 Function: O.isOption
episode_body:
Title: O.isOption
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Option
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: Guards

Description: Determines whether the given value is an `Option`. **When to use** - Validating unknown values at runtime boundaries - Type-narrowing in union types **Behavior** - Returns `true` for both `Some` and `None` instances - Acts as a type guard, narrowing the input to `Option<unknown>`

Signature: isOption: (input: unknown) => input is Option<unknown> = option.isOption

Example:
```ts
import { Option } from "effect"

console.log(O.isOption(O.some(1)))
// Output: true

console.log(O.isOption(O.none()))
// Output: true

console.log(O.isOption({}))
// Output: false
```

Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Function: O.isNone
episode_body:
Title: O.isNone
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Option
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: Guards

Description: Checks whether an `Option` is `None` (absent). **When to use** - Branching on absence before accessing `.value` **Behavior** - Acts as a type guard, narrowing to `None<A>`

Signature: isNone: <A>(self: Option<A>) => self is None<A> = option.isNone

Example:
```ts
import { Option } from "effect"

console.log(O.isNone(O.some(1)))
// Output: false

console.log(O.isNone(O.none()))
// Output: true
```

Title: O.isSome
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Option
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: Guards

Description: Checks whether an `Option` contains a value (`Some`). **When to use** - Branching on presence before accessing `.value` **Behavior** - Acts as a type guard, narrowing to `Some<A>`

Signature: isSome: <A>(self: Option<A>) => self is Some<A> = option.isSome

Example:
```ts
import { Option } from "effect"

console.log(O.isSome(O.some(1)))
// Output: true

console.log(O.isSome(O.none()))
```

name: Effect v4 Function: O.match
episode_body:
Title: O.match
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Option
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: Pattern matching

Description: Pattern-matches on an `Option`, handling both `None` and `Some` cases. **When to use** - Exhaustively handling both branches in one expression - Transforming an `Option` into a plain value **Behavior** - If `None`, calls `onNone` and returns its result - If `Some`, calls `onSome` with the value and returns its result - Supports the `dual` API (data-last and data-first)

Signature: match: { <B, A, C = B>(options: { readonly onNone: LazyArg<B> readonly onSome: (a: A) => C }): (self: Option<A>) => B | C

Example:
```ts
import { Option } from "effect"

const message = O.match(O.some(1), {
  onNone: () => "Option is empty",
  onSome: (value) => `Option has a value: ${value}`
})

console.log(message)
// Output: "Option has a value: 1"
```

Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Function: O.toRefinement
episode_body:
Title: O.toRefinement
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Option
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: Conversions

Description: Converts an `Option`-returning function into a type guard (refinement). **When to use** - Turning a parsing function into a type-narrowing predicate - Filtering arrays with `Array.prototype.filter` **Behavior** - Returns `true` when the original function returns `Some` - Returns `false` when the original function returns `None` - Narrows the input type to `B` on success

Signature: toRefinement = <A, B extends A>(f: (a: A) => Option<B>): (a: A) => a is B => (a: A): a is B => isSome(f(a))

Example:
```ts
import { Option } from "effect"

type MyData = string | number

const parseString = (data: MyData): O.Option<string> =>
  typeof data === "string" ? O.some(data) : O.none()

//      ┌─── (a: MyData) => a is string
//      ▼
const isString = O.toRefinement(parseString)

console.log(isString("a"))
// Output: true

console.log(isString(1))
// Output: false
```

Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Function: O.fromIterable
episode_body:
Title: O.fromIterable
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Option
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: Constructors

Description: Wraps the first element of an `Iterable` in a `Some`, or returns `None` if the iterable is empty. **When to use** - Safely extracting the head of a collection - Working with generators or lazy iterables **Behavior** - Only consumes the first element; does not iterate the rest - Returns `None` for empty iterables

Signature: fromIterable = <A>(collection: Iterable<A>): Option<A> => { for (const a of collection) { return some(a) } return none()

Example:
```ts
import { Option } from "effect"

console.log(O.fromIterable([1, 2, 3]))
// Output: { _id: 'Option', _tag: 'Some', value: 1 }

console.log(O.fromIterable([]))
// Output: { _id: 'Option', _tag: 'None' }
```

name: Effect v4 Function: O.getSuccess
episode_body:
Title: O.getSuccess
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Option
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: Conversions

Description: Converts a `Result` into an `Option`, keeping only the success value. **When to use** - Discarding the error channel when you only care about success **Behavior** - `Ok` → `Some` with the success value - `Err` → `None` (error is discarded)

Signature: getSuccess: <A, E>(self: Result<A, E>) => Option<A> = result.getSuccess

Example:
```ts
import { Option, Result } from "effect"

console.log(O.getSuccess(Result.succeed("ok")))
// Output: { _id: 'Option', _tag: 'Some', value: 'ok' }

console.log(O.getSuccess(Result.fail("err")))
// Output: { _id: 'Option', _tag: 'None' }
```

Module Path: effect/Option
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: Conversions

Signature: getFailure: <A, E>(self: Result<A, E>) => Option<E> = result.getFailure

name: Effect v4 Function: O.getOrElse
episode_body:
Title: O.getOrElse
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Option
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: Getters

Description: Extracts the value from a `Some`, or evaluates a fallback thunk on `None`. **When to use** - Providing a default value for an absent `Option` - Unwrapping with lazy evaluation of the fallback **Behavior** - `Some` → returns the inner value - `None` → calls `onNone()` and returns its result - `onNone` is only called when needed (lazy)

Signature: getOrElse: { <B>(onNone: LazyArg<B>): <A>(self: Option<A>) => B | A <A, B>(self: Option<A>, onNone: LazyArg<B>): A | B } = dual( 2,

Example:
```ts
import { Option } from "effect"

console.log(O.some(1).pipe(O.getOrElse(() => 0)))
// Output: 1

console.log(O.none().pipe(O.getOrElse(() => 0)))
// Output: 0
```

Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Function: O.orElse
episode_body:
Title: O.orElse
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Option
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: Error handling

Description: Returns the fallback `Option` if `self` is `None`; otherwise returns `self`. **When to use** - Chaining fallback `Option` computations - Building priority chains of optional values **Behavior** - `Some` → returns `self` unchanged - `None` → evaluates and returns `that()` - `that` is lazily evaluated

Signature: orElse: { <B>(that: LazyArg<Option<B>>): <A>(self: Option<A>) => Option<B | A> <A, B>(self: Option<A>, that: LazyArg<Option<B>>): Option<A | B> } = dual( 2,

Example:
```ts
import { Option } from "effect"

console.log(O.none().pipe(O.orElse(() => O.some("b"))))
// Output: { _id: 'Option', _tag: 'Some', value: 'b' }

console.log(O.some("a").pipe(O.orElse(() => O.some("b"))))
// Output: { _id: 'Option', _tag: 'Some', value: 'a' }
```

Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Function: O.orElseSome
episode_body:
Title: O.orElseSome
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Option
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: Error handling

Description: Returns `Some` of the fallback value if `self` is `None`; otherwise returns `self`. **When to use** - Providing a default plain value (not an `Option`) as fallback **Behavior** - `Some` → returns `self` unchanged - `None` → calls `onNone()`, wraps result in `Some`, and returns it

Signature: orElseSome: { <B>(onNone: LazyArg<B>): <A>(self: Option<A>) => Option<B | A> <A, B>(self: Option<A>, onNone: LazyArg<B>): Option<A | B> } = dual( 2,

Example:
```ts
import { Option } from "effect"

console.log(O.none().pipe(O.orElseSome(() => "b")))
// Output: { _id: 'Option', _tag: 'Some', value: 'b' }

console.log(O.some("a").pipe(O.orElseSome(() => "b")))
// Output: { _id: 'Option', _tag: 'Some', value: 'a' }
```

Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Function: O.orElseResult
episode_body:
Title: O.orElseResult
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Option
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: Error handling

Description: Like orElse, but wraps the result in a `Result` to indicate the source of the value. **When to use** - Distinguishing whether a value came from the primary or fallback `Option` **Behavior** - `self` is `Some` → `Some(Result.fail(value))` (value from primary) - `self` is `None`, `that()` is `Some` → `Some(Result.succeed(value))` (value from fallback) - Both `None` → `None`

Signature: orElseResult: { <B>(that: LazyArg<Option<B>>): <A>(self: Option<A>) => Option<Result<B, A>> <A, B>(self: Option<A>, that: LazyArg<Option<B>>): Option<Result<B, A>> } = dual( 2,

Example:
```ts
import { Option } from "effect"

console.log(O.orElseResult(O.some("primary"), () => O.some("fallback")))
// Output: { _id: 'Option', _tag: 'Some', value: { _tag: 'Failure', value: 'primary' } }

console.log(O.orElseResult(O.none(), () => O.some("fallback")))

```

Signature: map: { <A, B>(f: (a: A) => B): (self: Option<A>) => Option<B> <A, B>(self: Option<A>, f: (a: A) => B): Option<B> } = dual( 2,

Export Kind: const
Category Tag: Zipping

Description: Sequences two `Option`s, keeping the value from the second if both are `Some`. **When to use** - Running a side-condition that must succeed, then using the second value **Behavior** - Both `Some` → returns `that` - Either `None` → returns `None`

Signature: zipRight: { <B>(that: Option<B>): <_>(self: Option<_>) => Option<B> <X, B>(self: Option<X>, that: Option<B>): Option<B> } = dual(2, <X, B>(self: Option<X>, that: Option<B>): Option<B> => flatMap(self, () => that))

Example:
```ts
import { Option } from "effect"

console.log(O.zipRight(O.some(1), O.some("hello")))
// Output: { _id: 'Option', _tag: 'Some', value: 'hello' }

console.log(O.zipRight(O.none(), O.some("hello")))
// Output: { _id: 'Option', _tag: 'None' }
```

Signature: zipLeft: { <_>(that: Option<_>): <A>(self: Option<A>) => Option<A> <A, X>(self: Option<A>, that: Option<X>): Option<A> } = dual(2, <A, X>(self: Option<A>, that: Option<X>): Option<A> => tap(self, () => that))

```ts
import { Option } from "effect"
```

name: Effect v4 Function: O.composeK
episode_body:
Title: O.composeK
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Option
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: Sequencing

Description: Composes two `Option`-returning functions into a single function that chains them together. **When to use** - Building pipelines of partial functions (Kleisli composition) **Behavior** - Calls `afb(a)`, then if `Some`, calls `bfc` with its value - Short-circuits to `None` if either function returns `None`

Signature: composeK: { <B, C>(bfc: (b: B) => Option<C>): <A>(afb: (a: A) => Option<B>) => (a: A) => Option<C> <A, B, C>(afb: (a: A) => Option<B>, bfc: (b: B) => Option<C>): (a: A) => Option<C> } = dual(2, <A, B, C>(afb: (a: A) => Option<B>, bfc: (b: B) => Option<C>) => (a: A): Option<C> => flatMap(afb(a), bfc)

Example:
```ts
import { Option } from "effect"

const parse = (s: string): O.Option<number> =>
  isNaN(Number(s)) ? O.none() : O.some(Number(s))

const double = (n: number): O.Option<number> =>
  n > 0 ? O.some(n * 2) : O.none()

const parseAndDouble = O.composeK(parse, double)

console.log(parseAndDouble("42"))
// Output: { _id: 'Option', _tag: 'Some', value: 84 }

console.log(parseAndDouble("not a number"))
// Output: { _id: 'Option', _tag: 'None' }
```

name: Effect v4 Function: O.tap
episode_body:
Title: O.tap
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Option
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: Sequencing

Description: Runs a side-effecting `Option`-returning function on the value of a `Some`, returning the original `Option` if the function returns `Some`, or `None` if it returns `None`. **When to use** - Validating a value without transforming it - Adding a side-condition check in a pipeline **Behavior** - `None` → `None` - `Some` → calls `f(value)`; if result is `Some`, returns original `self`; if `None`, returns `None`

Signature: tap: { <A, X>(f: (a: A) => Option<X>): (self: Option<A>) => Option<A> <A, X>(self: Option<A>, f: (a: A) => Option<X>): Option<A> } = dual(2, <A, X>(self: Option<A>, f: (a: A) => Option<X>): Option<A> => flatMap(self, (a) => map(f(a), () => a)))

Example:
```ts
import { Option } from "effect"

const getInteger = (n: number) =>
  Number.isInteger(n) ? O.some(n) : O.none()

console.log(O.tap(O.some(1), getInteger))
// Output: { _id: 'Option', _tag: 'Some', value: 1 }

console.log(O.tap(O.some(1.14), getInteger))
// Output: { _id: 'Option', _tag: 'None' }
```

Title: O.product

Description: Combines two `Option`s into a `Some` containing a tuple `[A, B]` if both are `Some`. **When to use** - Pairing two optional values together **Behavior** - Both `Some` → `Some([a, b])` - Either `None` → `None`

Signature: product = <A, B>(self: Option<A>, that: Option<B>): Option<[A, B]> => isSome(self) && isSome(that) ? some([self.value, that.value]) : none()

Example:
```ts
import { Option } from "effect"

console.log(O.product(O.some("hello"), O.some(42)))
// Output: { _id: 'Option', _tag: 'Some', value: ['hello', 42] }

console.log(O.product(O.none(), O.some(42)))
// Output: { _id: 'Option', _tag: 'None' }
```

Description: Reduces an iterable of `Option`s to a single value, skipping `None` entries. **When to use** - Aggregating values from a collection where some may be absent **Behavior** - Iterates through the collection, applying `f` only to `Some` values - `None` values are skipped entirely - Returns the accumulated result

Signature: reduceCompact: { <B, A>(b: B, f: (b: B, a: A) => B): (self: Iterable<Option<A>>) => B <A, B>(self: Iterable<Option<A>>, b: B, f: (b: B, a: A) => B): B } = dual( 3,

episode_body:
Title: O.makeEquivalence
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Option
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: Equivalence

Description: Creates an `Equivalence` for `Option<A>` from an `Equivalence` for `A`. **When to use** - Comparing two `Option` values for structural equality **Behavior** - `None` vs `None` → `true` - `Some` vs `None` (or vice versa) → `false` - `Some(a)` vs `Some(b)` → delegates to the provided `Equivalence`

Signature: makeEquivalence = <A>(isEquivalent: Equivalence.Equivalence<A>): Equivalence.Equivalence<Option<A>> => Equivalence.make((x, y) => isNone(x) ? isNone(y) : isNone(y) ? false : isEquivalent(x.value, y.value))

Example:
```ts
import { Equivalence, Option } from "effect"

const eq = O.makeEquivalence(Equivalence.strictEqual<number>())

console.log(eq(O.some(1), O.some(1)))
// Output: true

console.log(eq(O.some(1), O.some(2)))
// Output: false

console.log(eq(O.none(), O.none()))
// Output: true
```

Since: 2.0.0

name: Effect v4 Function: O.makeOrder
episode_body:
Title: O.makeOrder
Category: function
Module Path: effect/Option
Export Kind: const
Category Tag: Sorting

Description: Creates an `Order` for `Option<A>` from an `Order` for `A`. **When to use** - Sorting collections of `Option` values **Behavior** - `None` is considered less than any `Some` - Two `Some` values are compared using the provided `Order` - Two `None` values are equal (returns `0`)

Signature: makeOrder = <A>(O: Order<A>): Order<Option<A>> => order.make((self, that) => isSome(self) ? (isSome(that) ? O(self.value, that.value) : 1) : -1)

Example:
```ts
import { Option } from "effect"
import * as N from "effect/Number"

const ord = O.makeOrder(N.Order)

console.log(ord(O.none(), O.some(1)))
// Output: -1

console.log(ord(O.some(1), O.none()))
// Output: 1

console.log(ord(O.some(1), O.some(2)))
// Output: -1
```

name: Effect v4 Function: O.lift2

Category: function
Module Path: effect/Option

Category Tag: Lifting

Description: Lifts a binary function to operate on two `Option` values. **When to use** - Reusing an existing binary function in an `Option` context **Behavior** - Both `Some` → applies `f` and wraps in `Some` - Either `None` → `None`

Signature: lift2 = <A, B, C>(f: (a: A, b: B) => C): { (that: Option<B>): (self: Option<A>) => Option<C> (self: Option<A>, that: Option<B>): Option<C> } => dual(2, (self: Option<A>, that: Option<B>): Option<C> => zipWith(self, that, f))

Example:
```ts
import { Option } from "effect"

const addOptions = O.lift2((a: number, b: number) => a + b)

console.log(addOptions(O.some(2), O.some(3)))
// Output: { _id: 'Option', _tag: 'Some', value: 5 }

console.log(addOptions(O.some(2), O.none()))
```

Description: Lifts a `Predicate` or `Refinement` into the `Option` context: returns `Some(value)` when the predicate holds, `None` otherwise. **When to use** - Converting a boolean check into an `Option`-returning function - Validating input and wrapping it in `Option` **Behavior** - `predicate(value)` is `true` → `Some(value)` - `predicate(value)` is `false` → `None` - Supports refinements for type narrowing

Signature: liftPredicate: { // Note: I intentionally avoid using the NoInfer pattern here. <A, B extends A>(refinement: Refinement<A, B>): (a: A) => Option<B> <B extends A, A = B>(predicate: Predicate<A>): (b: B) => Option<B> <A, B extends A>( self: A,

```ts
import { Option } from "effect"

const parsePositive = O.liftPredicate((n: number) => n > 0)

console.log(parsePositive(1))
// Output: { _id: 'Option', _tag: 'Some', value: 1 }

console.log(parsePositive(-1))
```

name: Effect v4 Function: O.containsWith
